response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a accomplished senior/staff web and full-stack engineer with 15+ years of experience, but the profile is fundamentally misaligned with this Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. His closest adjacent experience is low-latency software systems (async I/O, high-throughput HTTP pipelines) and some embedded/robotics work, but these do not constitute the hardware-level performance engineering the role demands.
strengths: 15+ years of continuous engineering experience satisfies the educational/experience threshold for a BS holder (8+ years required)
Demonstrated performance-conscious systems work at Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O, and caching techniques
Exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq shows awareness of modern AI workflows, even if at the software layer
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling — the primary technical requirement of the role
No background in GPU architecture, deep neural network training/inference optimization, or SIMD/MIMD architectures
Entire career is software application and web engineering; no evidence of hardware-software co-design or silicon-level performance analysis
